creator: joeb1@a1poweruser.com
text:
-----Original Message-----
From: Matthew Seaman [mailto:matthew@FreeBSD.org]
Sent: Sunday, February 02, 2014 6:14 PM
To: bug-followup@FreeBSD.org; jail-primer@a1poweruser.com
Subject: Re: ports/181945: [ New Port] sysutils/jail-primer



Hi, Joe,

Sorry about the delay in getting this new port added to the tree.  I'm
looking at it now.  Since you submitted the PR there have been some
changes to the ports, primarily the introduction of staging.  I've
adapted the port to account for all that, except for the LICENSE
setting. See:

    https://redports.org/buildarchive/20140202100800-19641/

There's one problem:

You have LICENSE=BSD in the port Makefile.  This needs to be updated to
say which variant on the BSD licence you are employing (ie. BSD2CLAUSE,
BSD3CLAUSE or BSD4CLAUSE).  However, you haven't actually included any
license text in your sources, so I don't know which of those you intended.

Could you add to the distfile tarball either a separate file
'copyright.txt' or as a comment at the top of each of your scripts
something like this:

# Copyright (c) 2014 Joe Barbish. All rights reserved.
#
# Redistribution and use in source and binary forms, with or without
# modification, are permitted provided that the following conditions
# are met:
#
#    1.  Redistributions of source code must retain the above
#        copyright notice, this list of conditions and the following
#        disclaimer.
#
#    2.  Redistributions in binary form must reproduce the above
#        copyright notice, this list of conditions and the following
#        disclaimer in the documentation and/or other materials
#        provided with the distribution.
#
# THIS SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED BY THE AUTHOR AND CONTRIBUTORS ``AS IS''
# AND ANY EXPRESS OR IMPLIED WARRANTIES, INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED
# TO, THE IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A
# PARTICULAR PURPOSE ARE DISCLAIMED. IN NO EVENT SHALL THE AUTHOR OR
# CONTRIBUTORS BE LIABLE FOR ANY DIRECT, INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL,
# SPECIAL, EXEMPLARY, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES (INCLUDING, BUT NOT
# LIMITED TO, PROCUREMENT OF SUBSTITUTE GOODS OR SERVICES; LOSS OF
# USE, DATA, OR PROFITS; OR BUSINESS INTERRUPTION) HOWEVER CAUSED AND
# ON ANY THEORY OF LIABILITY, WHETHER IN CONTRACT, STRICT LIABILITY,
# OR TORT (INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE OR OTHERWISE) ARISING IN ANY WAY OUT
# OF THE USE OF THIS SOFTWARE, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF
# SUCH DAMAGE.

That's obviously the two clause variant, which is generally preferred
nowadays.  If you would prefer a different variant, sample text can be
obtained here: http://opensource.org/licenses/category

This is one of those tedious legal necessities I'm afraid: you can't
just say your software is released under a certain copyright -- you have
to document it in the text of your software.

	Cheers,

	Matthew

--
Dr Matthew J Seaman MA, D.Phil.
PGP: http://www.infracaninophile.co.uk/pgpkey


Hi Matthew

I am currently working on contract outside of my home country until March
28.
I currently don't have the resources or time to address this until late
April.

Would changing the LICENSE=BSD in the port Makefile to LICENSE=public domain
be ok? Doing so would get the port committed now which is fine with me.

If not then I plan to use the BSD2CLAUSE BSD license and just add a
copyright.txt file
to the distfile tarball.
In what host directory should the copyright.txt file get written to during
the
make process?


Thanks
Joe
